items.title: Axalta automotive colors by year: 2015-2026
items.desc: A practical overview of Axalta global automotive colors from 2015 Radiant Red to 2026 Solar Boost.
items.sourceTitle: Axalta Color of the Year History
sections: Why this series matters
Axalta is one of the major coatings companies that identifies a global automotive color of the year based on market and design trends.
This series turns the 2015-2026 color history into practical notes for customers, paint matching and color conversations.
2015-2026 color list
The first group includes 2015 Radiant Red, 2016 Brilliant Blue, 2017 Gallant Gray, 2018 StarLite, 2019 Sahara and 2020 Sea Glass.
The recent group includes 2021 ElectroLight, 2022 Royal Magenta, 2023 Techno Blue, 2024 Starry Night, 2025 Evergreen Sprint and 2026 Solar Boost.
How Set Otomotiv uses it
These posts help explain color character, trend direction and the right questions to ask before paint selection.
The content is not a formula or a replacement for a physical color chart; sample panels and technical data should be checked before production.
sideNotes: 2015-2026 color series is grouped in one place.
A separate detail page was added for each year.
Physical color chart checks are still required.
items.source_name: Axalta Color of the Year History

items.title: Axalta 2026 Global Automotive Color: Solar Boost
items.desc: A practical Set Otomotiv note on Solar Boost, automotive color trends and paint application sensitivity.
items.sourceTitle: Axalta / Standox Color of the Year
sections: Color summary
Axalta highlighted Solar Boost for 2026. Its color character is: warm orange with champagne-like shimmer.
Solar Boost is a warm orange automotive color that highlights energy, optimism and personal expression on modern vehicles.
What it means for Set Otomotiv
Trend colors like Solar Boost are useful references when explaining color character and guiding special shade requests.
These colors should be evaluated together with lighting, panel angle and existing paint aging before local repair, part painting or full repaint decisions.
Application note
Its orange character can create a strong statement on sports cars, crossovers and special projects.
On metallic or glass-flake effects, panel angle, lighting and clearcoat quality can strongly affect the final look.
sideNotes: Trend colors help explain customer expectations.
Effect colors require sample panel checks.
Physical color charts remain important for final decisions.
items.source_name: Axalta / Standox Color of the Year

items.title: Axalta 2020 automotive color popularity report: key notes
items.desc: A practical summary of Axalta 68th Global Automotive Color Popularity Report from a paint and customer preference perspective.
sections: News summary
Axalta 68th Global Automotive Color Popularity Report shows that white remained a leading vehicle color globally, while black and gray also stayed among the top color families.
The report highlights the rise of gray and the decline of silver, reflecting a shift toward more modern and premium neutral tones.
Why it matters for Set Otomotiv customers
White, black and gray remain high-volume color families in repair and refinishing work. Correct code, variant and lighting checks are essential.
Gray color families often include metallic, pearl or effect variations. A color name alone is not enough for reliable matching.
Application note
Neutral colors can look easy, but white and gray variants can differ noticeably because of aging, panel position and effect pigment behavior.
notes: White, black and gray remain key repair colors.
Gray effect variations can change the result.
Paint code and variant verification matter.
items.source_name: Standox News Release

items.title: Axalta 2019 color popularity report: gray rises in Europe
items.desc: A Set Otomotiv summary of the gray, white, black and silver trends in Axalta 67th Global Automotive Color Popularity Report.
sections: News summary
Axalta 67th Global Automotive Color Popularity Report notes that white remained globally dominant, with black and gray also ranking strongly.
The report also points to gray becoming the leading automotive color in Europe for the first time, while silver continued to lose ground.
Why it matters for Set Otomotiv customers
European vehicles can have many gray variants. The same model may differ by year, plant, paint code and variant.
Gray color matching should combine the vehicle code, physical sample and spray-out panel rather than relying only on a digital swatch.
Application note
For metallic gray and silver, flake orientation, spray gun settings, coat thickness and clearcoat finish can change the perceived color.
notes: Gray has become a strong European trend.
Metallic orientation is critical in local repair.
Code, variant and spray-out checks should be combined.
items.source_name: Standox News Release
